author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.

On the words of the Gospel, John xii. 44, ‘He that believeth on me, believeth not on me, but on him that sent me.’ Against a certain expression of Maximinus, a bishop of the Arians, who spread his blasphemy in Africa where he was with the Count Segisvult.

ccel.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

title: On the words of the Gospel, John xii. 44, ‘He that believeth on me, believeth not on me, but on him that sent me.’ Against a certain expression of Maximinus, a bishop of the Arians, who spread his blasphemy in Africa where he was with the Count Segisvult.

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is it, Brethren, which we have heard the Lord saying, “He that believeth on Me, believeth not on Me, but on Him that sent Me”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It is good for us to believe on Christ, especially seeing that He hath also Himself expressly said this which ye have now heard, that is, that “He had come a Light into the world, and whosoever believeth on Him shall not walk in darkness, but shall have the light of life.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Good then it is to believe on Christ; and a great evil it is not to believe on Christ.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But because Christ the Son is, Whatsoever He is, of the Father, but the Father is not of the Son, but is the Father of the Son; He recommends to us indeed faith in Himself, but refers the honour to His Original.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For hold this fast as a firm and settled truth, if ye would continue Catholics, that God the Father begat God the Son without time, and made Him of a Virgin in time.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The first nativity exceedeth times; the second nativity enlighteneth times.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Yet both nativities are marvellous; the one without a mother, the other without a father.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When God begat the Son, He begat Him of Himself, not of a mother; when the Mother gave birth to her Son, she gave Him birth as a Virgin, not by man.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He was born of the Father without a beginning; He was born of a mother, as to-day at an appointed beginning.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Born of the Father He made us; born of a mother He re-made us.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He was born of the Father, that we might be; He was born of a mother, that we might not be lost.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But the Father begat Him equal to Himself, and All Whatsoever the Son is, He hath of the Father.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But What God the Father is, He hath not of the Son.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Accordingly we say that the Father is God, of none; the Son, God of God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Wherefore all that the Son doeth marvellously, all that He saith truly, He attributeth to Him of whom He is; yet can He not be ought else than He of whom He is.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Adam was made a man; he had power to become something other than he was made.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For he was made righteous, and he had power to become unrighteous.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But the Only-Begotten Son of God, What He is, This cannot be changed; He cannot be changed into anything else, cannot be diminished, What He was He cannot but be, He cannot but be equal to the Father.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But undoubtedly He who gave all things to the Son by His Birth, gave it to One not needing ought; without doubt this very equality too with the Father, the Father gave to the Son.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: How did the Father give It? did He beget Him less, and add to Him to complete His Form, that He might make Him equal?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If he had done this, He would have given it to one in need.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But I have told you already what ye ought most firmly to hold fast, that is, that All That the Son is, the Father gave Him, gave Him, that is, by His Birth, not as in need of ought.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If He gave it to Him by His Birth, and not as in need, then doubtless He both gave Him equality, and in giving Him equality, begat Him equal.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And although the One be One Person, and the Other Another; yet is not the One one thing, and the Other another; but What the One is, That the Other also.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He who is the One, is not the Other; but What the One, That too the Other.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “He Who sent Me,” saith He, ye have heard it; “He Who sent Me,” saith He, “He gave Me a commandment what I should say, and what I should speak; and I know that His commandment is life everlasting.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It is John’s Gospel, hold it fast.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “He Who sent Me, He gave Me a commandment what I should say, and what I should speak; and I know that His commandment is life everlasting.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: O that He would grant me to say what I wish!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For my poverty and His abundance straiteneth me.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “He,” saith He, “gave Me a commandment, what I should say, and what I should speak; and I know that His commandment is life everlasting.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Search in the Epistle of this John the Evangelist for what he hath said of Christ.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Let us believe,” he says, “His True Son Jesus Christ.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This is the True God and Everlasting Life.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is, “The True God, and Everlasting Life”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The True Son of God is the “the True God, and Everlasting Life.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Why did He say, “On His True Son”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Because God hath many sons, therefore was He to be distinguished, by adding that He was the True Son.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Not by simply saying that He is the Son; but by adding, as I have said, that He is the True Son; therefore He was to be distinguished, because of the many sons which God hath.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For we are sons by grace, He by Nature.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: We made by the Father through Him; He Himself That Which the Father is; are we too That Which God is?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But some man coming across us, knowing not what he is saying, says, “For this reason was it said, “I and My Father are One; for that They have with One Another an agreement of will, not because the Nature of the Son is the Very Same as the Nature of the Father.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For the Apostles too (now this is what he said, not I), for the Apostles too are one with the Father and the Son.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Horrible blasphemy!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “And the Apostles,” says he, “are one with the Father and the Son, in that they obey the will of the Father and the Son.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Has he dared to say this?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let Paul then say, “I and God are one.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let Peter say it, let every one of the Prophets say, “I and God are one.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They do not say it; God forbid they should.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They know that they are a different nature, a nature that needeth to be saved; they know that they are a different nature, a nature that needeth to be enlightened.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: No one says, “I and God are one.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Whatsoever progress he may make, howsoever he may surpass others in holiness, with how great eminence soever of virtue he may excel, he never saith, “I and God are one;” for if he have excellence, and therefore saith it; by saying it, he loseth what he had.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Believe then that the Son is equal with the Father; but yet that the Son is of the Father; but the Father not of the Son.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Original is with the Father, equality with the Son.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For if He be not equal, He is not a true Son.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For what are we saying, Brethren?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If He is not equal, He is less; if He is less, I ask the nature that needeth to be saved, in its misbelief, “how is He born less?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Answer, Doth He as being less grow or not?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If He groweth, then the Father groweth old.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But if He will ever be what He was born; if He was born less, He will continue less; with this His loss He will be perfect; born perfect with this loss of the Father’s Form, He is never to attain to the Father’s Form.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thus do ye ungodly assail the Son; thus do ye heretics blaspheme the Son.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What then saith the Catholic faith?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Son is God, of God the Father; God the Father, not God of the Son.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But God the Son equal with the Father, Born equal; not Born less, not made equal, but Born equal.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What the Father is, That is He also who was born.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Was the Father ever without the Son?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Take away your “ever,” where there is no time.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Father always, the Son always.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Father without beginning of time, the Son without beginning of time; the Father never before the Son, the Father never without the Son.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But yet because the Son is God of God the Father, and the Father God, but not of God the Son; let not the honouring of the Son in the Father displease us.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For the honouring of the Son giveth honour to the Father, it diminisheth not His Own Divinity.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Because then I was speaking of what I had brought forward, “And I knew,” saith He, “that His commandment is everlasting life.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Mark, Brethren, what I am saying; “I know that His commandment is everlasting life.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And we read in the same John concerning Christ, “He is The True God and Everlasting Life.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If the Father’s commandment is “everlasting Life,” and Christ the Son Himself is “everlasting Life;” the Son is Himself the Father’s Commandment.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For how is not That the Father’s Commandment, which is the Father’s Word?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Or if you take the commandment given to the Son by the Father in a carnal sense, as if the Father said to the Son, “I command Thee this, I wish Thee to do that;” in what words spake He to the Only Word?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When He gave commandment to the Word, did He look for words?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: That the Father’s Commandment then is “Life everlasting,” and that the Son Himself is “Life everlasting,” believe ye and receive, believe and understand, for the Prophet saith, “Unless ye believe ye shall not understand.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Do ye not comprehend?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Be enlarged.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Hear the Apostle: “Be ye enlarged, bear not the yoke with unbelievers.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They who will not believe this before they comprehend, are unbelievers.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And because they have determined to be unbelievers, they will remain in their ignorance.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let them believe then that they may understand.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Most certainly the Father’s Commandment is “everlasting Life.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore the Father’s Commandment is the Very Son who was born this day; a Commandment not given in time but a Commandment Born.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Gospel of John exercises our minds, refines and uncarnalizes them, that of God we may think not after a carnal but a spiritual manner.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let so much then, Brethren, suffice you; lest in length of disputation, the sleep of forgetfulness steal over you.
